theoremName: augmented closed-cone KLM-to-de Branges bridge theorem

canonicalHardyStatus.label: canonical Hardy/de Branges identity
canonicalHardyStatus.reason: The shifted-Xi de Branges kernel equals the signed Hardy branch Gram K_E=<h^+,h^+>-<h^-,h^->.

finiteThetaAugmentedPullbackStatus.label: finite theta augmented pullbacks
finiteThetaAugmentedPullbackStatus.reason: The analytic augmented pullback theorem proves that, for each finite theta truncation Xi_N, the exact Mellin split gives h_{z,N}^\pm as a Mu-boundary primitive plus diagonal Volterra tail.  This is the augmented pullback through R_aug=(Lambda,Mu), and its signed Hardy Gram is exactly K_{E,N}.

continuumAugmentedRepairStatus.label: continuum augmented trace repair
continuumAugmentedRepairStatus.reason: The continuum augmented repair theorem proves Mu is a closed trace coordinate, D_aug is bounded and nonnegative in the transported trace norm, and K+R_aug^*D_aug R_aug is a closed positive form.

uniformThetaTailStatus.label: uniform compact theta-tail convergence
uniformThetaTailStatus.reason: For |Im z|<=M, the n-th theta atom is bounded by C_M n^4 exp(-pi n^2/2).  Indeed, for v>=0, exp(-pi n^2 e^v)<=exp(-pi n^2/2)exp(-pi e^v/2), and the remaining v-integral is finite uniformly on the compact strip.  The Weierstrass M-test gives Xi_N->Xi uniformly on compact shifted z-sets, hence the branch Gram entries converge uniformly on finite test sets.

closedPositiveConeStatus.label: closed positive-cone limit
closedPositiveConeStatus.reason: For each finite test set, positive semidefinite Gram matrices form a closed cone.  Entrywise convergence of the finite augmented KLM pullback Grams to K_E therefore preserves positivity.

klmToDeBrangesBridgeStatus.label: KLM-to-de Branges closed-cone bridge
klmToDeBrangesBridgeStatus.reason: The finite augmented KLM pullbacks converge entrywise to the shifted-Xi de Branges kernel.  Since each finite pullback is in the KLM positive cone and the cone is closed on finite Gram matrices, K_E is positive on every finite evaluation set.

formalProof: For finite theta truncation N, define Xi_N from modes n<=N and E_{omega,N}(z)=Xi_N(z+i omega).
The exact Mellin split writes each h_{z,N}^± as a sum of a Mu-boundary primitive and a diagonal Volterra tail.  This is the augmented pullback through R_aug=(Lambda,Mu).
The continuum augmented repair theorem puts these finite pullbacks in the KLM positive cone on the completed augmented trace-fiber domain.
Uniform compact convergence Xi_N->Xi implies K_{E,N}(w,z)->K_E(w,z) entrywise for every finite evaluation set in the upper half-plane.
The finite positive semidefinite cone is closed; hence K_E is positive on every finite evaluation set.
nextProofTarget: Apply the de Branges/Hermite-Biehler conclusion for E_omega(z)=Xi(z+i omega) and record the RH-side endpoint passage.
